description:
Offered: Fall
Strong writing comes from good editing. This class explores different types of editing – developmental, line-editing, and proofreading – for the various stages in the editorial process. Students also learn about the relationship and collaboration between editors, writers and agents,- the submission and selection process, writing for special markets, and trends or practices in publishing. Students produce an issue of Route 2, the campus literary journal, and hone their editorial abilities through applied projects.
Prerequisite(s): COMM 3870 or ENGL 2020 or ENGL 2040 or ENGL 3510 or ENGL 3520 or ENGL 3870 or ENGL 3890
Meets General Education Requirement(s): IHIP
prerequisites: COMM 3870 or ENGL 2020 or ENGL 2040 or ENGL 3510 or ENGL 3520 or ENGL 3870 or ENGL 3890
